barriers.name: No Daytime Childcare
barriers.description: Cannot find or afford daytime childcare, preventing attendance at day-shift jobs or training programs.
barriers.playbook: Connect client with Texas Workforce Commission Child Care Services (CCS) subsidy and local Head Start centers. Verify eligibility for CCS through Workforce Solutions for Tarrant County and assist with application.

barriers.name: No Evening Childcare
barriers.description: Lack of evening/night childcare options limits availability for second-shift or overnight employment.
barriers.playbook: Identify licensed evening care providers in the Tarrant County area. Explore family support networks and employer-sponsored backup care options.

barriers.name: No Infant Care Available
barriers.description: Infant care slots are scarce and expensive, creating a barrier for parents of children under 12 months.
barriers.playbook: Check waitlists at licensed infant care centers. Connect with Early Head Start of Tarrant County and home-based care provider networks for immediate openings.

barriers.name: Unaware of Childcare Subsidies
barriers.description: Client does not know about available childcare assistance programs such as the Texas CCS subsidy.
barriers.playbook: Educate client on Texas Workforce Commission Child Care Services (CCS) subsidy eligibility. Walk through application process and provide contact info for Workforce Solutions for Tarrant County.

barriers.name: Childcare Unaffordable
barriers.description: Cost of childcare consumes too large a share of income, making employment financially unviable.
barriers.playbook: Calculate childcare cost vs. income ratio. Apply for sliding-scale fee programs and explore employer childcare benefits or co-op arrangements.

barriers.name: No Personal Vehicle
barriers.description: Client lacks access to a personal vehicle, limiting job opportunities to transit-accessible locations.
barriers.playbook: Map Trinity Metro routes from client's home to potential employers. Connect with vehicle assistance programs and explore carpool matching services.

barriers.name: No Public Transit Access
barriers.description: Client lives in an area with no or very limited public transit service, isolating them from employment centers.
barriers.playbook: Identify alternative transportation: Trinity Metro ZIPZONE on-demand service, vanpools, employer shuttles. Consider relocation assistance if housing is flexible.

barriers.name: No Night Shift Transportation
barriers.description: Public transit does not operate during night shift hours, preventing second and third shift employment.
barriers.playbook: Check employer willingness to adjust shifts. Explore ride-share stipends, employer shuttle programs, and bicycle commuting for short distances.

barriers.name: Unreliable Transportation
barriers.description: Vehicle breaks down frequently or transit is inconsistent, causing attendance problems at work.
barriers.playbook: Connect with auto repair assistance programs. Establish backup transportation plan with Trinity Metro, carpool, or ride-share as contingency.

barriers.name: Transportation Cost Too High
barriers.description: Gas, insurance, or transit pass costs consume a disproportionate share of income.
barriers.playbook: Calculate commute cost vs. income. Apply for Trinity Metro reduced-fare program, explore employer transit benefits, and consider closer employment options.

barriers.name: Low Credit Score
barriers.description: Credit score below 580 limits access to housing, auto loans, and some employment opportunities requiring credit checks.
barriers.playbook: Pull free credit report to identify specific issues. Create a credit improvement plan targeting highest-impact items first. Connect with credit counseling.

barriers.name: No Credit History
barriers.description: Client has no established credit file, making it difficult to access housing, utilities, or financing.
barriers.playbook: Open a secured credit card or credit-builder loan. Enroll in rent reporting programs to build credit history from existing payments.

barriers.name: High Debt Burden
barriers.description: Outstanding debt payments consume too much of monthly income, limiting financial stability.
barriers.playbook: Create debt inventory and prioritize by interest rate. Explore debt consolidation, income-driven repayment plans, and negotiate with creditors.

barriers.name: Accounts in Collections
barriers.description: One or more accounts have been sent to collections, damaging credit and causing wage garnishment risk.
barriers.playbook: Verify debt validity under FDCPA. Negotiate pay-for-delete agreements where possible. Connect with Legal Aid of NorthWest Texas for disputed or invalid debts.

barriers.name: No Bank Account
barriers.description: Client is unbanked, paying check-cashing fees and unable to receive direct deposit from employers.
barriers.playbook: Identify second-chance banking programs at local credit unions. Assist with opening a basic checking account to enable direct deposit.

barriers.name: Unstable Housing
barriers.description: Client is couch-surfing, in a shelter, or at risk of homelessness, making steady employment very difficult.
barriers.playbook: Connect with emergency housing programs and shelters. Apply for rapid rehousing assistance and establish stable address for employment applications.

barriers.name: Housing Unaffordable
barriers.description: Rent or mortgage exceeds 40% of income, leaving insufficient funds for other basic needs.
barriers.playbook: Apply for Section 8 or public housing waitlists with Fort Worth Housing Solutions. Explore shared housing options and negotiate with landlord for reduced rent or payment plan.

barriers.name: Eviction on Record
barriers.description: Past eviction makes it very difficult to pass tenant screening for new rental housing.
barriers.playbook: Connect with Legal Aid of NorthWest Texas to review eviction record for sealing or expungement eligibility. Identify landlords who accept applicants with eviction history.

barriers.name: No Health Insurance
barriers.description: Lack of health coverage means medical issues go untreated, potentially causing work absences.
barriers.playbook: Screen for Medicaid/CHIP eligibility and assist with enrollment. Check marketplace plans during open enrollment. Connect with JPS Health Network sliding-scale clinics for immediate care.

barriers.name: Physical Disability
barriers.description: Physical limitation requires workplace accommodations that not all employers can provide.
barriers.playbook: Document accommodation needs under ADA. Connect with Texas Workforce Solutions Vocational Rehabilitation for job placement support and workplace modification assistance.

barriers.name: Mental Health Challenges
barriers.description: Depression, anxiety, or other mental health conditions affect job performance and attendance.
barriers.playbook: Connect with mental health providers accepting Medicaid or sliding-scale fees (JPS Behavioral Health, MHMR of Tarrant County). Explore Employee Assistance Programs and workplace accommodation options.

barriers.name: Substance Use Disorder
barriers.description: Active or recent substance use issues affect employability and may require treatment before stable employment.
barriers.playbook: Connect with SAMHSA treatment locator and local recovery programs. Support treatment-compatible work schedules and sober living arrangements.

barriers.name: No Professional Certification
barriers.description: Client lacks industry certifications needed for higher-paying positions in their field.
barriers.playbook: Identify relevant certifications for target industry. Connect with WIOA-funded training programs (Workforce Solutions for Tarrant County) and scholarship opportunities for certification exams.

barriers.name: Skills Gap
barriers.description: Client's current skills do not match employer requirements in available job openings.
barriers.playbook: Conduct skills assessment to identify specific gaps. Enroll in targeted short-term training at Tarrant County College or workforce development center.

barriers.name: No GED or High School Diploma
barriers.description: Lack of high school credential eliminates many job opportunities and training program eligibility.
barriers.playbook: Enroll in GED preparation program at Tarrant County College or local adult education center. Explore employer-sponsored GED programs and online preparation resources.

barriers.name: Outdated Job Skills
barriers.description: Skills from previous employment are no longer relevant to current labor market demands.
barriers.playbook: Assess transferable skills and identify in-demand fields. Connect with retraining programs and apprenticeship opportunities in growing industries.

barriers.name: Criminal Record
barriers.description: Criminal conviction history limits employment options, especially with employers who run background checks.
barriers.playbook: Research ban-the-box and fair-chance employers in the Tarrant County area. Explore order of nondisclosure and expunction eligibility through Legal Aid of NorthWest Texas, and connect with reentry employment programs.

barriers.name: Background Check Barrier
barriers.description: Pending charges or recent record causes automatic disqualification from many employer screening processes.
barriers.playbook: Identify fair-chance employers who evaluate candidates individually. Prepare disclosure statement and gather character references for applications.

barriers.name: Reentry from Incarceration
barriers.description: Recently released from incarceration with gaps in work history, limited contacts, and reentry challenges.
barriers.playbook: Connect with reentry programs (Cornerstone Assistance Network, Catholic Charities Fort Worth) for job readiness training. Assist with ID documents, resume development, and transitional employment placement.

barriers.name: Extended Employment Gap
barriers.description: Gap of 6+ months in work history raises concerns with potential employers during hiring process.
barriers.playbook: Develop narrative explaining gap positively. Fill with volunteer work, training, or freelance experience. Practice interview responses for gap questions.

barriers.name: Low Income / Underemployment
barriers.description: Current earnings are below self-sufficiency level, creating cascading financial barriers.
barriers.playbook: Identify higher-paying opportunities matching client skills. Connect with career advancement programs and negotiate for raises or promotions at current employer.

barriers.name: Limited Work Hours Available
barriers.description: Constraints like childcare or transportation limit available work hours, reducing income potential.
barriers.playbook: Address root constraints first (childcare, transportation). Explore remote work options and employers offering flexible scheduling.

barriers.name: Job Instability
barriers.description: Pattern of short-term employment due to cascading barriers creates a cycle of instability.
barriers.playbook: Identify and address root-cause barriers creating instability. Build emergency fund plan and develop retention strategies with employer support.
